name: Platinum Coniferum
description:
Reshaping Marble Blast Platinum under my own view and leadership! The levels will either be twists on already existing concepts, or entirely new concepts. Expect most levels to be short to medium sized.

Recommended playing order + difficulties:
Beginner:
- Art of Rolling
- Jumping Trials 
- Hazardous Entrance
- Powerup House 
- Flip n' Bounce!
- Natural Sapphire
- Angelic Flight

Intermediate:
- Swift Introduction
- Tower of Persistence
- The Intergalactic Matrix Tube
- Grey Stroll
- Cosmic Escape

Advanced:
- Ruta Graciosa
- Evil Connie's Castle
- Battlecube Divided
- Ascension
- Icy Climb
- Speleothems
- Realm of Obelisks

Expert:
- Frostbound Bastion
- Grasshopper
- 

Master:
- 
- El Maestro X: The Final Climb (Planned)
- Last One Standing (Planned)

Battle Tetra, Gravity Tower, Marble City Classic, and Skate Battle Square from OpenMBU will be added to Director's Cut as well.

name: World 0 - beginner
description: Beginner where place for starter and people practicing and learning to play many people cannot make it in the first try because of beginner they learned many stuff such how to move the marble, power ups, hazard and other, people after completely learned much stuff, they can make it easily.

name: World 1 - Marbleland
description: After you managed to survive the "Marble Distractions" you decided to do a tightrope and you finish. You finally go into Marbleland, this is where starts to get tough. You go into level there many obstacles and it's sometimes very big and hard but these levels is possible to complete, each world have seven levels and the seventh (final level) cloud be boss level witch means it act like defeating boss with qualifying the levels some of the levels are has time limit, some of the levels are challenging, some of the levels are big you need to know how to qualify, you need to practice so you can make it easily.

name: World 2 - Ruins
description:
Through the old Buildings, Marble get trapped in a place where there old places, some of them have visitors, some of them is quiet, some of them is castle, many thing you can find, like just a ruins and you can make in easily! Not like the previous one, but this is really had many gems inside, but
hard part was feel annoying and after you through the castle, here you go, you finally make it through the ruins, in the next world, PREPARE TO BE A PLATFORMER MASTER, hey! You just accidentally made it on 0 seconds! Congratulations!

name: World 3 - Platformers
description: Marble get tired after solving puzzles in lost castle and face to face with Eden's flying apples on garden of Eden marble was now in the platform land witch marble must get trough by parkouring on these platforms. Do you know? Each world has their own theme from the name. Back to the story, marble decided to jump from platforms to platforms and them.... You, in this third world, you must have jumping skills and if tou don't have it, you can't make in easily but practice helps you to practicing more practicing and you make it easily.

name: PQ No TTs

name: Comeback
description:
Here's 10 latest PQ levels I've recently released on an impulse.
You can tell I was genuinely growing sick of letting my levels sleep in my folders, but since I was too focused on the idea of "making a level pack with a precise selection of levels that will revolutionize PQ", it cluttered my mind for a long time, until I finally decided to pull the trigger.

name: Oldies
description:
Here's the few PQ levels I've released before my big hiatus.
Quirky gameplay, new abstract skyboxes matching the spirit of PQ and fun concepts aplenty for your enjoyment!

name: Kula World in Marble Blast
description:
Kula World. This PS1 game kind of obsessed me, back in the day, with its very intuitive gameplay, the immersion this gem of a game offered with the 10 different worlds. So one day, I decided to try translating this experience in 10 MBP levels, themed after what I consider the grandpa of Marble Blast and most marble rolling games. 
Around 9 years ago, my level building abilities weren't that great at the time and I was experimenting a lot. 
Well, with the experience I have now, I can affirm that this pack was a hot freaking mess where I see only 2 good levels.
Maybe I'll give a (much better go) at this again in the future?

name: Old MBP levels
description:
Completely random themes? Levels named after girls? Painfully approximative grammar? 
In this pack, you will find the stuff I dared release around 10+ years ago!
A few good levels for a lot of cringe.

name: Marbler's Personal Favorites
description:
I've gathered my all-time favorite levels from some seriously talented creators like Nockess, Endy, Threefolder, and Connie—just to name a few. 
These legends didn’t just make levels; they totally crushed it, standing out from the usual Marbleland crowd and delivering a marbling experience like no other.

Wanna craft some epic levels yourself? Simple: steal their style, remix their magic, and make it your own. Copy the greats, then blow minds!

name: Roguelike Gem Hunt
description:
Welcome to Roguelike Gem Hunt.

Your score is now affected by multipliers which you gain by performing streaks.
Streaks are performed by collecting the colored gems in the following order:
Red Streak: 4 Reds
Yellow Streak: 4 Yellows
Blue Streak: 3 Blues
Platinum: 2 Platinums
Rainbow Streak: RYB, or BYR, in that order.
There is a timer which tells you how long your active multiplier lasts, which stacks if you perform streaks before the time runs out.

You can also upgrade various parts of the gameplay mechanics every time you level up.
Additionally, after every 5 levels, you can collect an artifact to spice things up.
Artifact effects are persistent when held unless described explicitly.

Your final score is calculated as:
First two digits: the level you reached.
Last two digits: percentage progress towards the next level.
So a score of 4215 would be interpreted as:
Reached level 42 with 15% progress towards the next level.

Gem value formula:
(Original Gem Value + Bonus Value) * Active

name: Marble Blast Levels: Generations
description:
A compilation of runs, at least one from each of a select few videos from the MBL series, improved years later. Potential video to commemorate the 15th anniversary of the series.

18 Gold, 2 Ultra, 24 Platinum, 3 PlatinumQuest, 1 Emerald, 1 Fubar, 1 Future

Levels not in marbleland:
1. Pitfalls
2. Timely Ascent (MBP)
3. Rising Fall
35. Wil o' Wisp (MBU)
41. Trap madness!
42. Temptation
47. Hawking's Dilemma

Videos not represented: 4, 6-7, 27, 30, 33

name: Connie's November 2025 Level Dump
description:
After (I think) an entire month or two with no levels from me, I decided to dedust some levels I've had sitting on my PC for quite a while and release all of them, alongside a new level I've been working on and teasing in the Marble Blast Community Server.

After a bit of polishing, I think they're ready to be shipped off to the world. Enjoy! (This doesn't mean I won't release more levels in November, though...)

name: Open MBU Hunt Customs

These levels were originally posted in the Open MBU Discord server, they have now been ported to PlatinumQuest.

An important notice: I did not make any of these levels, the only contributions I made were porting these over as well as deciding platinum/ ultimate scores. The only level not included is "Ultra Billiards" as it has complex custom code that was too hard to port over unfortunately.

name: (ID 1000) ILFC (Dozenalist)'s Levels
description:
Who loved this pack?

Also, LOOK AT THE URL! (Ha!)

To save you time, pack 10 and pack 100 are both nonexistent. Therefore, this pack has the first nontrivial power of ten ID that does not point to a deleted pack. Additionally, the pack has the first possible ID with four digits. I am lucky I managed to attain that.

If packs could be commented on, it is with a high degree of certainty that someone (NicolasV) would have commented regarding the ID milestone.

NOTE: I downloaded this pack once.
NOTE 2: I swapped this with the PQ pack, because I wanted the pack with ID 1000 to be the main one. You are not going insane.

name: Proper PlatinumQuest Non-Laps Non-Hunt Levels
description:
1. The level must be clearly PlatinumQuest-themed. (The Universes Colliding series by alexnico is an exception.)
2. No laps or hunt.
3. It must be a beatable level.
4. It cannot be a 'no gems' or 'no time travels' or 'NGTT' copy of a level.

name: Levels published in 2026 C.E. (120Xz) (I hate desc. char limit)
description:
Currently, you should likely disregard this pack and instead click the link below:
👉 https://marbleland.vaniverse.io/pack/975 👈
(Edit: Following such an instruction would exhibit recursive properties; you would eternally alternate between packs. This is because NicolasV has explicitly recommended to use my pack instead.)

However, 3 levels are missing from NicolasV’s pack:

• Red Trilogy
• (Unknown)
• Pablo's Lagoon Golf Course

Additionally, 2 levels were placed out-of-order in NicolasV's pack:

• The Pit
• Real Pads

(I am trying to help NicolasV with making his pack superior (or equivalent) to mine, rendering this pack obsolete and his pack more accurate than before.)

name: Levels published in 2025 C.E. (1209z)
description:
NicolasV stated that it is extremely detrimental to one’s mental health to create a ‘levels published in [ _ ]’ pack.

NicolasV failed to account for packs that are permanently complete, such as this one. This pack was arduous to create, but it requires no further maintenance because it is impossible to publish a level in a year that is no longer the current year in any time zone; that would require backward time travel.
